input.prompt:
I need to understand the structure and patterns used by existing channel plugins in the OpenClaw codebase. Please do a thorough exploration of the following:

1. Find all bundled workspace plugin directories (likely in a `plugins/` or `extensions/` subdirectory at the repo root). List them.
2. Pick 2-3 channel plugins (IRC, Nostr, Telegram if they exist) and read their full directory structure: what files they contain, their `package.json`, `openclaw.plugin.json` manifest, main entry file, and any key source files.
3. Read the plugin SDK contracts relevant to channels:
   - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`
   - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
   - `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts`
   - `src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts`
   - `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts`
   - `src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts`
4. Read any AGENTS.md in the bundled plugin tree and `src/plugin-sdk/AGENTS.md`
5. Look for any template or scaffold for new channel plugins.
6. Note any invariant tests that check plugin naming conventions (`src/plugins/contracts/registry.ts` or similar test files that enforce plugin id naming).

Please be thorough and report all file paths and their complete contents (or key excerpts for large files). I need this to write an implementation plan for a new "PulseBoard" channel plugin.

The goal is to create a faithful "PulseBoard" channel plugin that exactly follows the same patterns. Report all file paths and full contents.

input.description: Explore plugin SDK and channel type definitions
input.prompt:
I need to understand the plugin SDK and channel type system for OpenClaw to plan a new channel plugin. Please thoroughly read:

1. `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts` - full contents
2. `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts` - full contents
3. `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts` - full contents
4. `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts` - full contents (for contrast)
5. `src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts` - full contents
6. `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` - full contents  
7. `src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts` - full contents
8. `src/channels/plugins/AGENTS.md` or `src/channels/AGENTS.md` if it exists
9. Any `src/plugin-sdk/*.ts` files that look relevant to channel plugins
10. `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json` - full contents
11. Look for any `pnpm-workspace.yaml` or `package.json` at repo root that shows the workspace structure

Also search for how channels register their chat types (direct, group) and how they handle the `chunkLimit` or character limit setting.

Report all file paths with complete contents.

118	/** Options for a plugin entry that registers providers, tools, commands, or services. */
119	type DefinePluginEntryOptions = {
120	  id: string;
121	  name: string;
122	  description: string;
123	  kind?: OpenClawPluginDefinition["kind"];
124	  configSchema?: OpenClawPluginConfigSchema | (() => OpenClawPluginConfigSchema);
125	  register: (api: OpenClawPluginApi) => void;
126	};
127	
128	/** Normalized object shape that OpenClaw loads from a plugin entry module. */
129	type DefinedPluginEntry = {
130	  id: string;
131	  name: string;
132	  description: string;
133	  configSchema: OpenClawPluginConfigSchema;
134	  register: NonNullable<OpenClawPluginDefinition["register"]>;
135	} & Pick<OpenClawPluginDefinition, "kind">;
136	
137	/** Resolve either a concrete config schema or a lazy schema factory. */
138	function resolvePluginConfigSchema(
139	  configSchema: DefinePluginEntryOptions["configSchema"] = emptyPluginConfigSchema,
140	): OpenClawPluginConfigSchema {
141	  return typeof configSchema === "function" ? configSchema() : configSchema;
142	}
143	
144	/**
145	 * Canonical entry helper for non-channel plugins.
146	 *
147	 * Use this for provider, tool, command, service, memory, and context-engine
148	 * plugins. Channel plugins should use `defineChannelPluginEntry(...)` from
149	 * `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` so they inherit the channel capability wiring.
150	 */
151	export function definePluginEntry({
152	  id,
153	  name,
154	  description,
155	  kind,
156	  configSchema = emptyPluginConfigSchema,
157	  register,
158	}: DefinePluginEntryOptions): DefinedPluginEntry {
159	  return {
160	    id,
161	    name,
162	    description,
163	    ...(kind ? { kind } : {}),
164	    configSchema: resolvePluginConfigSchema(configSchema),
165	    register,
166	  };
167	}

1	# Channels Boundary
2	
3	`src/channels/**` is core channel implementation. Plugin authors should not
4	import from this tree directly.
5	
6	## Public Contracts
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
12	- Definition files:
13	  - `src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts`
14	  - `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts`
15	  - `src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts`
16	  - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
17	  - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`
18	
19	## Boundary Rules
20	
21	- Keep extension-facing channel surfaces flowing through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`
22	  instead of direct imports from `src/channels/**`.
23	- When a bundled or third-party channel needs a new seam, add a typed SDK
24	  contract or facade first.
25	- Remember that shared channel changes affect both built-in and extension
26	  channels. Check routing, pairing, allowlists, command gating, onboarding, and
27	  reply behavior across the full set.
28

134	  register: NonNullable<OpenClawPluginDefinition["register"]>;
135	} & Pick<OpenClawPluginDefinition, "kind">;

137	/** Resolve either a concrete config schema or a lazy schema factory. */
138	function resolvePluginConfigSchema(
139	  configSchema: DefinePluginEntryOptions["configSchema"] = emptyPluginConfigSchema,
140	): OpenClawPluginConfigSchema {
141	  return typeof configSchema === "function" ? configSchema() : configSchema;

10	/**
11	 * Validates https:// URLs only (no javascript:, data:, file:, etc.)
12	 */
13	const safeUrlSchema = z
14	  .string()
15	  .url()
16	  .refine(
17	    (url) => {
18	      try {
19	        const parsed = new URL(url);
20	        return parsed.protocol === "https:";
21	      } catch {
22	        return false;
23	      }
24	    },
25	    { message: "URL must use https:// protocol" },
26	  );
27	
28	/**
29	 * NIP-01 profile metadata schema
30	 * https://github.com/nostr-protocol/nips/blob/master/01.md
31	 */
32	export const NostrProfileSchema = z.object({
33	  /** Username (NIP-01: name) - max 256 chars */
34	  name: z.string().max(256).optional(),
35	
36	  /** Display name (NIP-01: display_name) - max 256 chars */
37	  displayName: z.string().max(256).optional(),
38	
39	  /** Bio/description (NIP-01: about) - max 2000 chars */
40	  about: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
41	
42	  /** Profile picture URL (must be https) */
43	  picture: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
44	
45	  /** Banner image URL (must be https) */
46	  banner: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
47	
48	  /** Website URL (must be https) */
49	  website: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
50	
51	  /** NIP-05 identifier (e.g., "user@example.com") */
52	  nip05: z.string().optional(),
53	
54	  /** Lightning address (LUD-16) */
55	  lud16: z.string().optional(),
56	});
57	
58	export type NostrProfile = z.infer<typeof NostrProfileSchema>;
59	
60	/**
61	 * Zod schema for channels.nostr.* configuration
62	 */
63	export const NostrConfigSchema = z.object({
64	  /** Account name (optional display name) */
65	  name: z.string().optional(),
66	
67	  /** Optional default account id for routing/account selection. */
68	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),
69	
70	  /** Whether this channel is enabled */
71	  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
72	
73	  /** Markdown formatting overrides (tables). */
74	  markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
75	
76	  /** Private key in hex or nsec bech32 format */
77	  privateKey: buildSecretInputSchema().optional(),
78	
79	  /** WebSocket relay URLs to connect to */
80	  relays: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
81	
82	  /** DM access policy: pairing, allowlist, open, or disabled */
83	  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional(),
84	
85	  /** Allowed sender pubkeys (npub or hex format) */
86	  allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema,
87	
88	  /** Profile metadata (NIP-01 kind:0 content) */
89	  profile: NostrProfileSchema.optional(),
90	});
91	
92	export type NostrConfig = z.infer<typeof NostrConfigSchema>;
93	
94	/**
95	 * JSON Schema for Control UI (converted from Zod)
96	 */
97	export const nostrChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(NostrConfigSchema);

1	# Extensions Boundary
2	
3	This directory contains bundled plugins. Treat it as the same boundary that
4	third-party plugins see.
5	
6	## Public Contracts
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
12	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-entrypoints.md`
13	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`
14	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md`
15	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md`
16	  - `docs/plugins/manifest.md`
17	- Definition files:
18	  - `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts`
19	  - `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
20	  - `src/plugin-sdk/provider-entry.ts`
21	  - `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts`
22	  - `scripts/lib/plugin-sdk-entrypoints.json`
23	  - `package.json`
24	
25	## Boundary Rules
26	
27	- Extension production code should import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` and its
28	  own local barrels such as `./api.ts` and `./runtime-api.ts`.
29	- Do not import core internals from `src/**`, `src/channels/**`,
30	  `src/plugin-sdk-internal/**`, or another extension's `src/**`.
31	- Do not use relative imports that escape the current extension package root.
32	- Keep plugin metadata accurate in `openclaw.plugin.json` and the package
33	  `openclaw` block so discovery and setup work without executing plugin code.
34	- Treat files like `src/**`, `onboard.ts`, and other local helpers as private
35	  unless you intentionally promote them through `api.ts` and, if needed, a
36	  matching `src/plugin-sdk/<id>.ts` facade.
37	- If core or core tests need a bundled plugin helper, export it from `api.ts`
38	  first instead of letting them deep-import extension internals.
39	
40	## Expanding The Boundary
41	
42	- If an extension needs a new seam, add a typed Plugin SDK subpath or additive
43	  export instead of reaching into core.
44	- Keep new plugin-facing seams backwards-compatible and versioned. Third-party
45	  plugins consume this surface.
46	- When intentionally expanding the contract, update the docs, exported subpath
47	  list, package exports, and API/contract checks in the same change.
48

1	# Plugins Boundary
2	
3	This directory owns plugin discovery, manifest validation, loading, registry
4	assembly, and contract enforcement.
5	
6	## Public Contracts
7	
8	- Docs:
9	  - `docs/plugins/architecture.md`
10	  - `docs/plugins/manifest.md`
11	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`
12	  - `docs/plugins/sdk-entrypoints.md`
13	- Definition files:
14	  - `src/plugins/types.ts`
15	  - `src/plugins/runtime/types.ts`
16	  - `src/plugins/contracts/registry.ts`
17	  - `src/plugins/public-artifacts.ts`
18	
19	## Boundary Rules
20	
21	- Preserve manifest-first behavior: discovery, config validation, and setup
22	  should work from metadata before plugin runtime executes.
23	- Keep loader behavior aligned with the documented Plugin SDK and manifest
24	  contracts. Do not create private backdoors that bundled plugins can use but
25	  external plugins cannot.
26	- If a loader or registry change affects plugin authors, update the public SDK,
27	  docs, and contract tests instead of relying on incidental internals.
28	- Do not normalize "plugin-owned" into "core-owned" by scattering direct reads
29	  of `plugins.entries.<id>.config` through unrelated core paths. Prefer generic
30	  helpers, plugin runtime hooks, manifest metadata, and explicit auto-enable
31	  wiring.
32	- When plugin-owned tools or provider fallbacks need core participation, keep
33	  the contract generic and honor plugin disablement plus SecretRef semantics.
34	- Keep contract loading and contract tests on the dedicated bundled registry
35	  path. Do not make contract validation depend on activating providers through
36	  unrelated production resolution flows.
37

31	 */
32	export const NostrProfileSchema = z.object({
33	  /** Username (NIP-01: name) - max 256 chars */
34	  name: z.string().max(256).optional(),
35	
36	  /** Display name (NIP-01: display_name) - max 256 chars */
37	  displayName: z.string().max(256).optional(),
38	
39	  /** Bio/description (NIP-01: about) - max 2000 chars */
40	  about: z.string().max(2000).optional(),
41	
42	  /** Profile picture URL (must be https) */
43	  picture: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
44	
45	  /** Banner image URL (must be https) */
46	  banner: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
47	
48	  /** Website URL (must be https) */
49	  website: safeUrlSchema.optional(),
50	
51	  /** NIP-05 identifier (e.g., "user@example.com") */
52	  nip05: z.string().optional(),
53	
54	  /** Lightning address (LUD-16) */

Perfect! Now I have enough information to compile a comprehensive report. Let me create the final analysis:

## Summary

I've completed a thorough exploration of the OpenClaw codebase and the channel plugin architecture. Here's my comprehensive report:

---

## 1. BUNDLED WORKSPACE PLUGIN DIRECTORIES

**Location**: `/extensions/` at repo root

**Complete List of Channel Plugins Found**:
- IRC (`irc`)
- Nostr (`nostr`)
- Telegram (`telegram`)
- Discord (`discord`)
- Slack (`slack`)
- Signal (`signal`)
- Matrix (`matrix`)
- WhatsApp (`whatsapp`)
- Line (`line`)
- Feishu (`feishu`)
- Mattermost (`mattermost`)
- Googlechat (`googlechat`)
- Msteams (`msteams`)
- Twitch (`twitch`)
- Nextcloud Talk (`nextcloud-talk`)
- Synology Chat (`synology-chat`)
- Zalo/ZaloUser (`zalo`, `zalouser`)
- BlueBubbles (`bluebubbles`)
- iMessage (`imessage`)
- Tlon (`tlon`)

**Total Extensions**: 94 directories (not all are channel plugins; some are providers, tools, or core services)

---

## 2. REPRESENTATIVE CHANNEL PLUGINS: DETAILED STRUCTURE

### A. IRC Plugin

**Path**: `/extensions/irc/`

**Files & Structure**:
```
irc/
├── package.json                      # npm package definition
├── openclaw.plugin.json              # Plugin manifest
├── index.ts                          # Main entry point
├── setup-entry.ts                    # Setup-only entry
├── api.ts                            # Public API barrel
├── channel-config-api.ts
└── src/
    ├── channel.ts                    # ChannelPlugin implementation
    ├── runtime.ts                    # Runtime store
    ├── accounts.ts                   # Account resolution
    ├── config-schema.ts              # Zod config schema
    ├── types.ts                      # Type definitions
    ├── setup-surface.ts              # Setup UI wizard
    ├── setup-core.ts                 # Setup adapter logic
    ├── runtime-api.ts                # Runtime helper exports
    ├── client.ts                     # IRC protocol client
    ├── send.ts                       # Outbound messaging
    ├── inbound.ts                    # Inbound routing
    ├── policy.ts                     # Group/security policies
    ├── normalize.ts                  # Target normalization
    ├── probe.ts                      # Account probing
    ├── monitor.ts                    # Status monitoring
    ├── config-ui-hints.ts            # UI schema hints
    └── [tests]                       # *.test.ts files
```

**package.json Key Fields**:
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/irc",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "irc",
      "label": "IRC",
      "selectionLabel": "IRC (Server + Nick)",
      "docsPath": "/channels/irc",
      "blurb": "classic IRC networks..."
    },
    "install": { "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1" }
  }
}
```

**openclaw.plugin.json**:
```json
{
  "id": "irc",
  "channels": ["irc"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

**index.ts Pattern**:
```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "irc",
  name: "IRC",
  description: "IRC channel plugin",
  plugin: ircPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setIrcRuntime,
});
```

**setup-entry.ts Pattern**:
```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(ircPlugin);
```

### B. Telegram Plugin

**Path**: `/extensions/telegram/`

**Key Differences from IRC**:
- More complex with dependencies: `grammy` (Telegram bot framework), `@grammyjs/runner`, `@grammyjs/transformer-throttler`
- Larger source tree with specialized modules for threading, approvals, buttons, reactions
- `bundle.stageRuntimeDependencies: true` in package.json
- Separate `channel.setup.ts` for setup-specific definition
- Rich API exports in `api.ts` (20+ exports)
- Setup-entry references `channel.setup.js` instead of `channel.js`
- Includes advanced features: inline buttons, reactions, approvals, threaded conversations

**Files** (>80 files):
```
telegram/
├── package.json
├── openclaw.plugin.json
├── index.ts
├── setup-entry.ts
├── api.ts                    # Large API surface

├── session-key-api.ts
├── allow-from.ts
└── src/

    ├── probe.ts

    ├── audit.ts
    ├── send.ts
    ├── outbound-adapter.ts
    ├── outbound-params.ts
    ├── normalize.ts
    ├── targets.ts
    ├── directory-config.ts
    ├── sticker-cache.ts
    ├── status-issues.ts
    └── [tests]
```

### C. Nostr Plugin

**Path**: `/extensions/nostr/`

**Key Characteristics**:
- Decentralized protocol, uses `nostr-tools` library
- Simpler than Telegram, similar to IRC baseline
- No external runtime dependencies (nostr-tools is bundled)
- Features NIP-04 encrypted DMs
- Includes HTTP handler for profile management via gateway
- Custom `registerFull()` implementation in index.ts for HTTP routes

**Unique Files**:
```
nostr/
├── runtime-api.ts
├── src/
    ├── channel.ts
    ├── runtime.ts

    ├── nostr-profile.ts       # Profile publishing
    ├── nostr-profile-http.ts  # HTTP endpoints for UI
    ├── metrics.ts
    ├── session-route.ts
    └── [tests]
```

---

## 3. PLUGIN SDK CONTRACTS & TYPES

### File Structure

**Core SDK Files**:
- `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts` (585+ lines) - Main entry point helper
- `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts` - Re-exports channel types
- `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts` - Generic plugin entry definitions
- `src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts` - ChannelPlugin contract
- `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` - Adapters & core types
- `src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts` - Adapter contracts

**Key Channel SDK Exports from `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`**:

1. **defineChannelPluginEntry()** - Channel plugin entry helper
   - Parameters: `id`, `name`, `description`, `plugin`, `configSchema?`, `setRuntime?`, `registerCliMetadata?`, `registerFull?`
   - Handles registration in different modes: cli-metadata, setup-only, full
   - Sets runtime before plugin registration
   - Calls `api.registerChannel({ plugin })`

2. **createChatChannelPlugin()** - Compose common chat patterns
   - Builds: DM security, text pairing, reply threading, attached send results
   - Resolves helpers from adapter interfaces
   - Returns complete ChannelPlugin

3. **createChannelPluginBase()** - Minimal base builder
   - Collects optional adapters: capabilities, threading, outbound, security, pairing, etc.
   - Auto-fills channel meta from SDK defaults

4. **defineSetupPluginEntry()** - Minimal setup entry wrapper
   - Simple: `{ plugin }`

### ChannelPlugin Contract (`types.plugin.ts`)

```typescript
export type ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount = any, Probe = unknown, Audit = unknown> = {
  id: ChannelId;
  meta: ChannelMeta;
  capabilities: ChannelCapabilities;
  defaults?: { queue?: { debounceMs?: number } };
  reload?: { configPrefixes: string[]; noopPrefixes?: string[] };
  setupWizard?: ChannelPluginSetupWizard;
  config: ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedAccount>;
  configSchema?: ChannelConfigSchema;
  setup?: ChannelSetupAdapter;
  pairing?: ChannelPairingAdapter;
  security?: ChannelSecurityAdapter<ResolvedAccount>;
  groups?: ChannelGroupAdapter;
  mentions?: ChannelMentionAdapter;
  outbound?: ChannelOutboundAdapter;
  status?: ChannelStatusAdapter<ResolvedAccount, Probe, Audit>;
  // ... many more optional adapters
};
```

### Key Adapter Types

**ChannelConfigAdapter**:
- `listAccountIds(cfg) -> string[]`
- `resolveAccount(cfg, accountId?) -> ResolvedAccount`
- `isConfigured(account) -> boolean`
- `isEnabled(account) -> boolean`
- `resolveAllowFrom(params) -> Array<string|number>`
- `defaultAccountId(cfg) -> string`

**ChannelOutboundAdapter**:
- `sendText(ctx) -> Promise<OutboundDeliveryResult>`
- `sendMedia(ctx) -> Promise<OutboundDeliveryResult>`
- `sendPoll(ctx) -> Promise<ChannelPollResult>`
- `resolveOutboundSessionRoute(params) -> ChannelOutboundSessionRoute`

**ChannelSecurityAdapter**:
- `resolveDmPolicy(ctx) -> ChannelSecurityDmPolicy`
- `collectWarnings(params) -> string[]`

**ChannelSetupAdapter**:
- `resolveAccountId(params) -> string`
- `applyAccountConfig(params) -> OpenClawConfig`
- `validateInput(params) -> string | null`
- `afterAccountConfigWritten(params) -> Promise<void>`

### ChannelMeta & Capabilities

```typescript
export type ChannelMeta = {
  id: ChannelId;
  label: string;
  selectionLabel: string;
  docsPath: string;
  blurb: string;
  order?: number;
  systemImage?: string;
  markdownCapable?: boolean;
  quickstartAllowFrom?: boolean;
  aliases?: readonly string[];
};

export type ChannelCapabilities = {
  inbound?: ChannelInboundCapability;
  messaging?: ChannelMessagingAdapter;
  approval?: ChannelApprovalCapability;
  // ... many more
};
```

---

## 4. PLUGIN SDK DOCUMENTATION FILES

**Documentation Paths**:
- `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md` - Plugin SDK overview
- `docs/plugins/sdk-entrypoints.md` - Entry point definitions
- `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md` - Runtime lifecycle
- `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` - Channel-specific contracts
- `docs/plugins/architecture.md` - Overall architecture
- `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md` - Plugin development guide
- `docs/plugins/manifest.md` - Plugin manifest specification

**Boundary Documentation**:
- `extensions/AGENTS.md` - Extension boundary rules & contracts
- `src/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md` - SDK boundary rules & expansion guidelines
- `src/channels/CLAUDE.md` - Channel boundary & public contracts

**Key Boundary Rules**:

1. **Extension authors MUST**:
   - Import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` (not core internals)
   - Use `./api.ts` as public barrel for shared helpers
   - Use relative imports within plugin only
   - Keep `openclaw.plugin.json` & package.json metadata accurate
   - Not import from `src/**`, `src/channels/**`, or other extensions

2. **Core/tests MAY**:
   - Import bundled plugin helpers from plugin `api.ts` instead of deep-importing `src/**`
   - Request new SDK seams if needed (not backdoors)

---

## 5. AGENTS.md & PLUGIN DOCUMENTATION

**extensions/AGENTS.md** - Documents:
- Plugin as third-party boundary
- Public contracts: docs, definition files (SDK paths)
- Boundary rules for imports and metadata
- Expansion guidelines for new seams

**src/plugins/AGENTS.md** - Documents:
- Manifest-first discovery behavior
- Loader/registry alignment with SDK
- Plugin-owned config vs core-owned
- Contract loading & testing

No special third-party plugin templates found; plugins follow same patterns as bundled.

---

## 6. PLUGIN NAMING CONVENTIONS & INVARIANT TESTS

**File**: `src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts`

**Naming Rules**:

1. **Directory Name** → `openclaw.plugin.json:id`
   - Exception: "kimi-coding" → id="kimi" (historical)

2. **Package Name** → `@openclaw/<id>` + optional suffix
   - **Allowed suffixes**: "" (none), "-provider", "-plugin", "-speech", "-sandbox", "-media-understanding"
   - Example: "@openclaw/telegram", "@openclaw/openai-provider"

3. **Package Name** → `openclaw.install.npmSpec` (if bundled & external)
   - Must match exactly for install determinism

4. **Channel ID** → `openclaw.plugin.json:id`
   - `openclaw.channel.id` must equal plugin id

**Test Invariants**:
- Bundle plugin directory ≈ manifest id (unless in DIR_ID_EXCEPTIONS)
- Bundle package name matches allowed patterns
- npmSpec matches package name
- channel.id matches manifest id

**Code Reference**:
```typescript
const DIR_ID_EXCEPTIONS = new Map<string, string>([
  ["kimi-coding", "kimi"],
]);

const ALLOWED_PACKAGE_SUFFIXES = [
  "", "-provider", "-plugin", "-speech", "-sandbox", "-media-understanding"
];
```

---

## 7. CHANNEL PLUGIN IDS REGISTRY

**File**: `src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.ts`

**Functions**:
- `resolveChannelPluginIds(params)` → All channel plugins available
- `resolveConfiguredChannelPluginIds(params)` → Installed & configured
- `resolveConfiguredDeferredChannelPluginIds(params)` → Deferred startup
- `resolveGatewayStartupPluginIds(params)` → Startup-required plugins

**Discovery**:
- Loads manifest registry
- Filters by `plugin.channels.length > 0`
- Maps to `plugin.id`

---

## 8. RUNTIME PATTERNS

### Runtime Store Pattern

All plugins use centralized runtime store:

```typescript
import { createPluginRuntimeStore } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store";

const { setRuntime, getRuntime } = createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("Error message");
export { getRuntime, setRuntime };
```

**Runtime Signature** (`PluginRuntime` from SDK):
```typescript
type PluginRuntime = {
  config: {
    loadConfig: () => OpenClawConfig;
    writeConfigFile: (cfg: OpenClawConfig) => Promise<void>;
  };
  logger: PluginLogger;
  // ... other shared fields
};
```

### Channel-Specific Runtime

Telegram extends runtime with channel-specific overrides:
```typescript
export type TelegramRuntime = PluginRuntime & {
  channel: PluginRuntime["channel"] & {
    telegram?: {
      probeTelegram?: ...,
      sendMessageTelegram?: ...,
      // ... overridable functions
    };
  };
};
```

---

## 9. ENTRY POINT PATTERNS

### Main Entry (index.ts)

```typescript
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { MyPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setMyRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "my-channel",
  name: "My Channel",
  description: "...",
  plugin: MyPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setMyRuntime,
  registerFull?: (api) => { /* gate-specific routes, tools */ },
  registerCliMetadata?: (api) => { /* CLI-only registration */ },
});
```

### Setup Entry (setup-entry.ts)

Minimal, setup-only definition:
```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { MySetupPlugin } from "./src/channel.setup.js";  // or "./src/channel.js"

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(MySetupPlugin);
```

### Configuration Schema

Uses Zod for validation + `buildChannelConfigSchema()` for JSON schema:

```typescript
import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
import { buildChannelConfigSchema } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives";

const MyConfigSchema = z.object({
  token: z.string(),
  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional(),
  allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema,
});

export const myChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(MyConfigSchema);
```

---

## 10. KEY SDK HELPER FUNCTIONS

**Available from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`**:

1. **stripChannelTargetPrefix(raw, ...providers)** - Remove provider prefixes
2. **stripTargetKindPrefix(raw)** - Remove user:/group: prefixes
3. **buildChannelOutboundSessionRoute(params)** - Build routing payload
4. **createInlineTextPairingAdapter(params)** - Simple text pairing
5. **emptyPluginConfigSchema** - No-op schema
6. **buildPluginConfigSchema()** - Zod to JSON schema
7. **getChatChannelMeta()** - Fetch channel metadata
8. **normalizeAccountId()** - Account ID normalization
9. **resolveThreadSessionKeys()** - Thread routing
10. **createSubsystemLogger()** - Scoped logging

**Helper Imports**:
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers` - Config adapters
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing` - Pairing builders
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-policy` - Security warnings
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/routing` - Session key builders
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers` - Status adapters
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/outbound-runtime` - Send dependencies

---

## 11. COMPLETE FILE PATHS

### Plugin SDK Contracts
- `/src/plugin-sdk/core.ts` (585 lines) - Main SDK facade
- `/src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts` - Channel type re-exports
- `/src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts` - Generic plugin entry
- `/src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts` - ChannelPlugin contract
- `/src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` - Adapter types
- `/src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts` - All adapter contracts

### Bundled Plugins (Sample)
- `/extensions/irc/` (38 files)
- `/extensions/telegram/` (80+ files)
- `/extensions/nostr/` (30+ files)
- `/extensions/discord/` (48 files)
- `/extensions/slack/` (35 files)
- `/extensions/signal/` (45 files)

### Plugin Infrastructure
- `/src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts` - Naming invariants
- `/src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.ts` - Registry helpers
- `/src/plugins/manifest-registry.ts` - Manifest loading
- `/src/plugins/types.ts` - Plugin type definitions

### Boundary Documents
- `/extensions/AGENTS.md` - Extension boundary
- `/src/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md` - SDK boundary
- `/src/channels/CLAUDE.md` - Channel boundary
- `/src/plugins/AGENTS.md` - Plugin infrastructure boundary

---

## IMPLEMENTATION PLAN FOR "PULSEBOARD" CHANNEL PLUGIN

Based on the above analysis, here's what you need to implement:

### 1. **Directory & Package Setup**
   - Create `/extensions/pulseboard/`
   - Create `package.json` with `name: "@openclaw/pulseboard"`, version, `type: "module"`
   - Create `openclaw.plugin.json` with `id: "pulseboard"`, `channels: ["pulseboard"]`
   - Simple empty `configSchema` initially

### 2. **Entry Points** (minimum 2 files)
   - `index.ts` - Call `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with plugin, setRuntime, optional handlers
   - `setup-entry.ts` - Call `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseboardPlugin)`

### 3. **Core Implementation** (`src/`)
   - `channel.ts` - Implement ChannelPlugin (call `createChatChannelPlugin()` if chat-style)
   - `runtime.ts` - Create runtime store with `createPluginRuntimeStore()`
   - `accounts.ts` - Implement account resolution (list, resolve, defaults)
   - `config-schema.ts` - Zod schema + `buildChannelConfigSchema()`
   - `setup-surface.ts` - SetupWizard (optional, for onboarding UI)
   - `setup-core.ts` - SetupAdapter (required)
   - `types.ts` - Type definitions for ResolvedPulseboardAccount
   - `normalize.ts` - Target ID normalization
   - `send.ts` - Outbound send implementation
   - `inbound.ts` - Inbound message routing (if applicable)
   - `probe.ts` - Account validation/probing
   - `monitor.ts` - Status monitoring

### 4. **Public API** (`api.ts`)
   - Export account helpers, setup helpers, types
   - Re-export from `src/` submodules (like Telegram's pattern)

### 5. **Configuration Adapter**
   - Implement `ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedPulseboardAccount>`
   - Methods: `listAccountIds`, `resolveAccount`, `isConfigured`, `isEnabled`, `resolveAllowFrom`, etc.

### 6. **Optional Capabilities**
   - **Outbound**: `ChannelOutboundAdapter` with `sendText`, `sendMedia`, `sendPoll`
   - **Security**: `ChannelSecurityAdapter` with `resolveDmPolicy`, `collectWarnings`
   - **Pairing**: `ChannelPairingAdapter` for approval notification
   - **Threading**: `ChannelThreadingAdapter` if supporting threaded conversations
   - **Status**: `ChannelStatusAdapter` for health/diagnostics

### 7. **Tests**
   - Mirror IRC/Telegram structure: `src/*.test.ts` files
   - Test config schema, accounts, normalize, send, etc.

### 8. **Naming Compliance**
   - Directory: `pulseboard` (matches id)
   - Package: `@openclaw/pulseboard` (matches id)
   - Channel ID: `pulseboard` (in openclaw.plugin.json)
   - npm spec: (if external) = `@openclaw/pulseboard`

---

**You now have everything needed to understand and implement a channel plugin for OpenClaw!**

```
    ├── monitor.ts
    ├── audit.ts
    ├── send.ts
    ├── outbound-adapter.ts
    ├── outbound-params.ts
    ├── normalize.ts
    ├── targets.ts
    ├── directory-config.ts
    ├── sticker-cache.ts
    ├── status-issues.ts
    └── [tests]
```

### C. Nostr Plugin

**Path**: `/extensions/nostr/`

**Key Characteristics**:
- Decentralized protocol, uses `nostr-tools` library
- Simpler than Telegram, similar to IRC baseline
- No external runtime dependencies (nostr-tools is bundled)
- Features NIP-04 encrypted DMs
- Includes HTTP handler for profile management via gateway
- Custom `registerFull()` implementation in index.ts for HTTP routes

**Unique Files**:
```
nostr/
├── runtime-api.ts
├── src/
    ├── channel.ts
    ├── runtime.ts
    ├── config-schema.ts       # Includes NIP-01 profile schema
    ├── types.ts
    ├── setup-surface.ts
    ├── setup-core.ts
    ├── nostr-bus.ts           # Event bus abstraction
    ├── nostr-profile.ts       # Profile publishing
    ├── nostr-profile-http.ts  # HTTP endpoints for UI
    ├── metrics.ts
    ├── session-route.ts
    └── [tests]
```

---

## 3. PLUGIN SDK CONTRACTS & TYPES

### File Structure

**Core SDK Files**:
- `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts` (585+ lines) - Main entry point helper
- `src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts` - Re-exports channel types
- `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts` - Generic plugin entry definitions
- `src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts` - ChannelPlugin contract
- `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` - Adapters & core types
- `src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts` - Adapter contracts

**Key Channel SDK Exports from `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`**:

1. **defineChannelPluginEntry()** - Channel plugin entry helper
   - Parameters: `id`, `name`, `description`, `plugin`, `configSchema?`, `setRuntime?`, `registerCliMetadata?`, `registerFull?`
   - Handles registration in different modes: cli-metadata, setup-only, full
   - Sets runtime before plugin registration
   - Calls `api.registerChannel({ plugin })`

2. **createChatChannelPlugin()** - Compose common chat patterns
   - Builds: DM security, text pairing, reply threading, attached send results
   - Resolves helpers from adapter interfaces
   - Returns complete ChannelPlugin

3. **createChannelPluginBase()** - Minimal base builder
   - Collects optional adapters: capabilities, threading, outbound, security, pairing, etc.
   - Auto-fills channel meta from SDK defaults

4. **defineSetupPluginEntry()** - Minimal setup entry wrapper
   - Simple: `{ plugin }`

### ChannelPlugin Contract (`types.plugin.ts`)

```typescript
export type ChannelPlugin<ResolvedAccount = any, Probe = unknown, Audit = unknown> = {
  id: ChannelId;
  meta: ChannelMeta;
  capabilities: ChannelCapabilities;
  defaults?: { queue?: { debounceMs?: number } };
  reload?: { configPrefixes: string[]; noopPrefixes?: string[] };
  setupWizard?: ChannelPluginSetupWizard;
  config: ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedAccount>;
  configSchema?: ChannelConfigSchema;
  setup?: ChannelSetupAdapter;
  pairing?: ChannelPairingAdapter;
  security?: ChannelSecurityAdapter<ResolvedAccount>;
  groups?: ChannelGroupAdapter;
  mentions?: ChannelMentionAdapter;
  outbound?: ChannelOutboundAdapter;
  status?: ChannelStatusAdapter<ResolvedAccount, Probe, Audit>;
  // ... many more optional adapters
};
```

### Key Adapter Types

**ChannelConfigAdapter**:
- `listAccountIds(cfg) -> string[]`
- `resolveAccount(cfg, accountId?) -> ResolvedAccount`
- `isConfigured(account) -> boolean`
- `isEnabled(account) -> boolean`
- `resolveAllowFrom(params) -> Array<string|number>`
- `defaultAccountId(cfg) -> string`

**ChannelOutboundAdapter**:
- `sendText(ctx) -> Promise<OutboundDeliveryResult>`
- `sendMedia(ctx) -> Promise<OutboundDeliveryResult>`
- `sendPoll(ctx) -> Promise<ChannelPollResult>`
- `resolveOutboundSessionRoute(params) -> ChannelOutboundSessionRoute`

**ChannelSecurityAdapter**:
- `resolveDmPolicy(ctx) -> ChannelSecurityDmPolicy`
- `collectWarnings(params) -> string[]`

**ChannelSetupAdapter**:
- `resolveAccountId(params) -> string`
- `applyAccountConfig(params) -> OpenClawConfig`
- `validateInput(params) -> string | null`
- `afterAccountConfigWritten(params) -> Promise<void>`

### ChannelMeta & Capabilities

```typescript
export type ChannelMeta = {
  id: ChannelId;
  label: string;
  selectionLabel: string;
  docsPath: string;
  blurb: string;
  order?: number;
  systemImage?: string;
  markdownCapable?: boolean;
  quickstartAllowFrom?: boolean;
  aliases?: readonly string[];
};

export type ChannelCapabilities = {
  inbound?: ChannelInboundCapability;
  messaging?: ChannelMessagingAdapter;
  approval?: ChannelApprovalCapability;
  // ... many more
};
```

---

## 4. PLUGIN SDK DOCUMENTATION FILES

**Documentation Paths**:
- `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md` - Plugin SDK overview
- `docs/plugins/sdk-entrypoints.md` - Entry point definitions
- `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md` - Runtime lifecycle
- `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` - Channel-specific contracts
- `docs/plugins/architecture.md` - Overall architecture
- `docs/plugins/building-plugins.md` - Plugin development guide
- `docs/plugins/manifest.md` - Plugin manifest specification

**Boundary Documentation**:
- `extensions/AGENTS.md` - Extension boundary rules & contracts
- `src/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md` - SDK boundary rules & expansion guidelines
- `src/channels/CLAUDE.md` - Channel boundary & public contracts

**Key Boundary Rules**:

1. **Extension authors MUST**:
   - Import from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` (not core internals)
   - Use `./api.ts` as public barrel for shared helpers
   - Use relative imports within plugin only
   - Keep `openclaw.plugin.json` & package.json metadata accurate
   - Not import from `src/**`, `src/channels/**`, or other extensions

2. **Core/tests MAY**:
   - Import bundled plugin helpers from plugin `api.ts` instead of deep-importing `src/**`
   - Request new SDK seams if needed (not backdoors)

---

## 5. AGENTS.md & PLUGIN DOCUMENTATION

**extensions/AGENTS.md** - Documents:
- Plugin as third-party boundary
- Public contracts: docs, definition files (SDK paths)
- Boundary rules for imports and metadata
- Expansion guidelines for new seams

**src/plugins/AGENTS.md** - Documents:
- Manifest-first discovery behavior
- Loader/registry alignment with SDK
- Plugin-owned config vs core-owned
- Contract loading & testing

No special third-party plugin templates found; plugins follow same patterns as bundled.

---

## 6. PLUGIN NAMING CONVENTIONS & INVARIANT TESTS

**File**: `src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts`

**Naming Rules**:

1. **Directory Name** → `openclaw.plugin.json:id`
   - Exception: "kimi-coding" → id="kimi" (historical)

2. **Package Name** → `@openclaw/<id>` + optional suffix
   - **Allowed suffixes**: "" (none), "-provider", "-plugin", "-speech", "-sandbox", "-media-understanding"
   - Example: "@openclaw/telegram", "@openclaw/openai-provider"

3. **Package Name** → `openclaw.install.npmSpec` (if bundled & external)
   - Must match exactly for install determinism

4. **Channel ID** → `openclaw.plugin.json:id`
   - `openclaw.channel.id` must equal plugin id

**Test Invariants**:
- Bundle plugin directory ≈ manifest id (unless in DIR_ID_EXCEPTIONS)
- Bundle package name matches allowed patterns
- npmSpec matches package name
- channel.id matches manifest id

**Code Reference**:
```typescript
const DIR_ID_EXCEPTIONS = new Map<string, string>([
  ["kimi-coding", "kimi"],
]);

const ALLOWED_PACKAGE_SUFFIXES = [
  "", "-provider", "-plugin", "-speech", "-sandbox", "-media-understanding"
];
```

---

## 7. CHANNEL PLUGIN IDS REGISTRY

**File**: `src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.ts`

**Functions**:
- `resolveChannelPluginIds(params)` → All channel plugins available
- `resolveConfiguredChannelPluginIds(params)` → Installed & configured
- `resolveConfiguredDeferredChannelPluginIds(params)` → Deferred startup
- `resolveGatewayStartupPluginIds(params)` → Startup-required plugins

**Discovery**:
- Loads manifest registry
- Filters by `plugin.channels.length > 0`
- Maps to `plugin.id`

---

## 8. RUNTIME PATTERNS

### Runtime Store Pattern

All plugins use centralized runtime store:

```typescript
import { createPluginRuntimeStore } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store";

const { setRuntime, getRuntime } = createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("Error message");
export { getRuntime, setRuntime };
```

**Runtime Signature** (`PluginRuntime` from SDK):
```typescript
type PluginRuntime = {
  config: {
    loadConfig: () => OpenClawConfig;
    writeConfigFile: (cfg: OpenClawConfig) => Promise<void>;
  };
  logger: PluginLogger;
  // ... other shared fields
};
```

### Channel-Specific Runtime

Telegram extends runtime with channel-specific overrides:
```typescript
export type TelegramRuntime = PluginRuntime & {
  channel: PluginRuntime["channel"] & {
    telegram?: {
      probeTelegram?: ...,
      sendMessageTelegram?: ...,
      // ... overridable functions
    };
  };
};
```

---

## 9. ENTRY POINT PATTERNS

### Main Entry (index.ts)

```typescript
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { MyPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setMyRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "my-channel",
  name: "My Channel",
  description: "...",
  plugin: MyPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setMyRuntime,
  registerFull?: (api) => { /* gate-specific routes, tools */ },
  registerCliMetadata?: (api) => { /* CLI-only registration */ },
});
```

### Setup Entry (setup-entry.ts)

Minimal, setup-only definition:
```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { MySetupPlugin } from "./src/channel.setup.js";  // or "./src/channel.js"

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(MySetupPlugin);
```

### Configuration Schema

Uses Zod for validation + `buildChannelConfigSchema()` for JSON schema:

```typescript
import { z } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod";
import { buildChannelConfigSchema } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-primitives";

const MyConfigSchema = z.object({
  token: z.string(),
  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
  dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional(),
  allowFrom: AllowFromListSchema,
});

export const myChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(MyConfigSchema);
```

---

## 10. KEY SDK HELPER FUNCTIONS

**Available from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`**:

1. **stripChannelTargetPrefix(raw, ...providers)** - Remove provider prefixes
2. **stripTargetKindPrefix(raw)** - Remove user:/group: prefixes
3. **buildChannelOutboundSessionRoute(params)** - Build routing payload
4. **createInlineTextPairingAdapter(params)** - Simple text pairing
5. **emptyPluginConfigSchema** - No-op schema
6. **buildPluginConfigSchema()** - Zod to JSON schema
7. **getChatChannelMeta()** - Fetch channel metadata
8. **normalizeAccountId()** - Account ID normalization
9. **resolveThreadSessionKeys()** - Thread routing
10. **createSubsystemLogger()** - Scoped logging

**Helper Imports**:
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-helpers` - Config adapters
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-pairing` - Pairing builders
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-policy` - Security warnings
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/routing` - Session key builders
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers` - Status adapters
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/outbound-runtime` - Send dependencies

---

## 11. COMPLETE FILE PATHS

### Plugin SDK Contracts
- `/src/plugin-sdk/core.ts` (585 lines) - Main SDK facade
- `/src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts` - Channel type re-exports
- `/src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts` - Generic plugin entry
- `/src/channels/plugins/types.plugin.ts` - ChannelPlugin contract
- `/src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` - Adapter types
- `/src/channels/plugins/types.adapters.ts` - All adapter contracts

### Bundled Plugins (Sample)
- `/extensions/irc/` (38 files)
- `/extensions/telegram/` (80+ files)
- `/extensions/nostr/` (30+ files)
- `/extensions/discord/` (48 files)
- `/extensions/slack/` (35 files)
- `/extensions/signal/` (45 files)

### Plugin Infrastructure
- `/src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts` - Naming invariants
- `/src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.ts` - Registry helpers
- `/src/plugins/manifest-registry.ts` - Manifest loading
- `/src/plugins/types.ts` - Plugin type definitions

### Boundary Documents
- `/extensions/AGENTS.md` - Extension boundary
- `/src/plugin-sdk/CLAUDE.md` - SDK boundary
- `/src/channels/CLAUDE.md` - Channel boundary
- `/src/plugins/AGENTS.md` - Plugin infrastructure boundary

---

## IMPLEMENTATION PLAN FOR "PULSEBOARD" CHANNEL PLUGIN

Based on the above analysis, here's what you need to implement:

### 1. **Directory & Package Setup**
   - Create `/extensions/pulseboard/`
   - Create `package.json` with `name: "@openclaw/pulseboard"`, version, `type: "module"`
   - Create `openclaw.plugin.json` with `id: "pulseboard"`, `channels: ["pulseboard"]`
   - Simple empty `configSchema` initially

### 2. **Entry Points** (minimum 2 files)
   - `index.ts` - Call `defineChannelPluginEntry()` with plugin, setRuntime, optional handlers
   - `setup-entry.ts` - Call `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseboardPlugin)`

### 3. **Core Implementation** (`src/`)
   - `channel.ts` - Implement ChannelPlugin (call `createChatChannelPlugin()` if chat-style)
   - `runtime.ts` - Create runtime store with `createPluginRuntimeStore()`
   - `accounts.ts` - Implement account resolution (list, resolve, defaults)
   - `config-schema.ts` - Zod schema + `buildChannelConfigSchema()`
   - `setup-surface.ts` - SetupWizard (optional, for onboarding UI)
   - `setup-core.ts` - SetupAdapter (required)
   - `types.ts` - Type definitions for ResolvedPulseboardAccount
   - `normalize.ts` - Target ID normalization
   - `send.ts` - Outbound send implementation
   - `inbound.ts` - Inbound message routing (if applicable)
   - `probe.ts` - Account validation/probing
   - `monitor.ts` - Status monitoring

### 4. **Public API** (`api.ts`)
   - Export account helpers, setup helpers, types
   - Re-export from `src/` submodules (like Telegram's pattern)

### 5. **Configuration Adapter**
   - Implement `ChannelConfigAdapter<ResolvedPulseboardAccount>`
   - Methods: `listAccountIds`, `resolveAccount`, `isConfigured`, `isEnabled`, `resolveAllowFrom`, etc.

### 6. **Optional Capabilities**
   - **Outbound**: `ChannelOutboundAdapter` with `sendText`, `sendMedia`, `sendPoll`
   - **Security**: `ChannelSecurityAdapter` with `resolveDmPolicy`, `collectWarnings`
   - **Pairing**: `ChannelPairingAdapter` for approval notification
   - **Threading**: `ChannelThreadingAdapter` if supporting threaded conversations
   - **Status**: `ChannelStatusAdapter` for health/diagnostics

### 7. **Tests**
   - Mirror IRC/Telegram structure: `src/*.test.ts` files
   - Test config schema, accounts, normalize, send, etc.

### 8. **Naming Compliance**
   - Directory: `pulseboard` (matches id)
   - Package: `@openclaw/pulseboard` (matches id)
   - Channel ID: `pulseboard` (in openclaw.plugin.json)
   - npm spec: (if external) = `@openclaw/pulseboard`

---

**You now have everything needed to understand and implement a channel plugin for OpenClaw!**

input.content:
# PulseBoard Channel Plugin — Implementation Plan

## Context

OpenClaw supports multiple messaging channels through a plugin/extension system. Each channel plugin lives under `extensions/<id>/` and implements the `ChannelPlugin` contract from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`. We need to add a new fictional "PulseBoard" channel — an imaginary real-time bulletin board platform where users post to named boards (group spaces, identified by `!boardname`) and send direct messages to each other. The plugin must support both `"direct"` and `"group"` chat types with a 4 000-character text chunk limit.

The implementation follows the same structure as the IRC plugin (the simplest complete channel plugin), with no modifications to any existing files. All 16 new files live exclusively under `extensions/pulseboard/`.

---

## Naming Invariants (enforced by `src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts`)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Directory | `extensions/pulseboard/` |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` → `id` | `"pulseboard"` |
| `package.json` → `name` | `"@openclaw/pulseboard"` |
| `package.json` → `openclaw.channel.id` | `"pulseboard"` |
| `npmSpec` | omit (bundled, not external) |

---

## File-by-File Plan

### 1. `extensions/pulseboard/package.json`
Mirror IRC's structure exactly. Key fields:
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/pulseboard",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw PulseBoard channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "install": { "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1" },
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "pulseboard",
      "label": "PulseBoard",
      "selectionLabel": "PulseBoard (Server + Token)",
      "detailLabel": "PulseBoard",
      "docsPath": "/channels/pulseboard",
      "docsLabel": "pulseboard",
      "blurb": "real-time bulletin board messaging with named boards and direct messages.",
      "aliases": ["pulse-board"],
      "systemImage": "rectangle.grid.2x2"
    }
  }
}
```
No extra `dependencies` block needed (no third-party runtime deps for a fictional protocol).

---

### 2. `extensions/pulseboard/openclaw.plugin.json`
```json
{
  "id": "pulseboard",
  "channels": ["pulseboard"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

---

### 3. `extensions/pulseboard/index.ts`
Main entry point — mirrors `extensions/irc/index.ts`.
```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseboardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setPulseboardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { pulseboardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setPulseboardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "pulseboard",
  name: "PulseBoard",
  description: "PulseBoard channel plugin",
  plugin: pulseboardPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setPulseboardRuntime,
});
```

---

### 4. `extensions/pulseboard/setup-entry.ts`
Minimal setup-only entry — mirrors `extensions/irc/setup-entry.ts`.
```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseboardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseboardPlugin);
```

---

### 5. `extensions/pulseboard/api.ts`
Public API barrel (same two-line pattern as `extensions/irc/api.ts`):
```typescript
export * from "./src/accounts.js";
export * from "./src/setup-surface.js";
```

---

### 6. `extensions/pulseboard/src/types.ts`
Defines all PulseBoard-specific TypeScript types. Key types:

- **`PulseboardAccountConfig`** — per-account config stored in `channels.pulseboard.*`:
  - `name?`, `enabled?`, `serverUrl?`, `apiToken?`, `username?`, `boards?: string[]`,
    `dmPolicy?`, `allowFrom?`, `defaultTo?`, `textChunkLimit?`
- **`PulseboardConfig`** — top-level, extends account config with `accounts?` and `defaultAccount?`
- **`CoreConfig`** — `OpenClawConfig & { channels?: { pulseboard?: PulseboardConfig } }`
- **`PulseboardInboundMessage`** — `{ messageId, boardId: string|null, senderId, text, timestamp, isDirect }`
- **`PulseboardProbe`** — `BaseProbeResult<string> & { serverUrl, username, latencyMs? }`

Import `OpenClawConfig` and `BaseProbeResult` from `./runtime-api.js`; import `DmPolicy` etc. from `./runtime-api.js`.

---

### 7. `extensions/pulseboard/src/config-schema.ts`
Zod schema + `buildChannelConfigSchema`. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts`:

- Import `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape` from
  `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema`
- Import `z` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod`
- Define `PulseboardAccountSchemaBase` (strict) with all config fields
- `PulseboardConfigSchema` extends base with `accounts` and `defaultAccount`
- Export `PulseboardChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(PulseboardConfigSchema)`

---

### 8. `extensions/pulseboard/src/accounts.ts`
Account resolution. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts`:

- Import `createAccountListHelpers` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers`
- Import `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id`
- Import `resolveMergedAccountConfig` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-resolution`
- Import `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input`

Key exports:
- `ResolvedPulseboardAccount` type — `{ accountId, enabled, name?, configured, serverUrl, username, apiToken, apiTokenSource: "env"|"config"|"none", config }`
- `listPulseboardAccountIds(cfg)` — via `createAccountListHelpers("pulseboard", ...)`
- `resolveDefaultPulseboardAccountId(cfg)` — via same helper
- `resolvePulseboardAccount({ cfg, accountId? })` — merges config, checks env vars
  (`PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME`, `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN`), returns resolved account.
  `configured = Boolean(serverUrl && username)`
- `listEnabledPulseboardAccounts(cfg)` — filters enabled accounts

---

### 9. `extensions/pulseboard/src/normalize.ts`
Target normalization for PulseBoard. Boards are prefixed with `!`, DMs use bare usernames.

Key exports:
- `isBoardTarget(target: string): boolean` — `target.startsWith("!")`
- `normalizePulseboardMessagingTarget(raw: string): string | undefined`
  - strips `"pulseboard:"` prefix
  - strips `"board:"` prefix, prepends `!` if missing
  - strips `"user:"` prefix
  - validates with `looksLikePulseboardTargetId`
- `looksLikePulseboardTargetId(raw: string): boolean`
  - boards: `!` + `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+`
  - users: `[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+`
- `normalizePulseboardAllowEntry(raw: string): string`
  - lowercases, strips `"pulseboard:"` and `"user:"` prefixes
- `normalizePulseboardAllowlist(entries?): string[]`

---

### 10. `extensions/pulseboard/src/runtime.ts`
Runtime store. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/runtime.ts` exactly (3 lines of logic):
```typescript
import { createPluginRuntimeStore } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store";
import type { PluginRuntime } from "./runtime-api.js";

const { setRuntime: setPulseboardRuntime, getRuntime: getPulseboardRuntime } =
  createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized");
export { getPulseboardRuntime, setPulseboardRuntime };
export function clearPulseboardRuntime() {
  setPulseboardRuntime(undefined as unknown as PluginRuntime);
}
```

---

### 11. `extensions/pulseboard/src/runtime-api.ts`
Private runtime barrel. Since there is no dedicated `openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard` facade,
re-export from the relevant general SDK subpaths directly. Needed by `channel.ts`, `runtime.ts`,
`send.ts`, `probe.ts`, and `types.ts`:

```typescript
// Private runtime barrel for the PulseBoard extension.
export {
  buildBaseChannelStatusSummary,
  createAccountStatusSink,
  createDefaultChannelRuntimeState,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers";
export { DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/routing";
export {
  chunkTextForOutbound,
  getChatChannelMeta,
  PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
export type {
  BaseProbeResult,
  ChannelPlugin,
  OpenClawConfig,
  PluginRuntime,
  DmPolicy,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
```

---

### 12. `extensions/pulseboard/src/send.ts`
Outbound send. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/send.ts`:

- `SendPulseboardResult = { messageId: string; target: string }`
- `sendMessagePulseboard(to, text, opts?)`:
  1. Load config from runtime or opts
  2. Call `resolvePulseboardAccount`
  3. Validate configured
  4. `normalizePulseboardMessagingTarget(to)` — throw if invalid
  5. Stub the HTTP call: in a real implementation this would `POST /api/messages` to
     `account.serverUrl` with `Authorization: Bearer <apiToken>` header
  6. Generate `messageId` with `makePulseboardMessageId()` — `pb-${Date.now()}-<random>`
  7. Call `runtime.channel.activity.record(...)` (wrapped in try/catch same as IRC)
  8. Return `{ messageId, target }`

---

### 13. `extensions/pulseboard/src/probe.ts`
Connectivity probe. Pattern from IRC's `probe.ts`:

- `probePulseboard(cfg, { accountId?, timeoutMs? }): Promise<PulseboardProbe>`
  - Resolve account; if not configured return `{ ok: false, error: "...", serverUrl, username }`
  - Stub: in a real implementation would `HEAD <serverUrl>/api/health` with a timeout
  - Return `{ ok: true, serverUrl, username, latencyMs: undefined }`

---

### 14. `extensions/pulseboard/src/setup-core.ts`
Setup adapter + config helpers. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/setup-core.ts`:

Imports from:
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-setup` — `ChannelSetupAdapter`, `ChannelSetupInput`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime` — `DmPolicy`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/routing` — `normalizeAccountId`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup` — `applyAccountNameToChannelSection`,
  `createSetupInputPresenceValidator`, `createTopLevelChannelAllowFromSetter`,
  `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicySetter`, `patchScopedAccountConfig`

Key exports:
- `updatePulseboardAccountConfig(cfg, accountId, patch)` — via `patchScopedAccountConfig`
- `setPulseboardDmPolicy(cfg, dmPolicy)` — via `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicySetter`
- `setPulseboardAllowFrom(cfg, allowFrom)` — via `createTopLevelChannelAllowFromSetter`
- `pulseboardSetupAdapter: ChannelSetupAdapter`:
  - `resolveAccountId`: `normalizeAccountId(accountId)`
  - `applyAccountName`: `applyAccountNameToChannelSection`
  - `validateInput`: `createSetupInputPresenceValidator` requiring `serverUrl`/`apiKey` and `token`/`userId`
  - `applyAccountConfig`: applies `serverUrl` (from `input.apiKey`), `apiToken` (from `input.token`), `username` (from `input.userId`) into config

---

### 15. `extensions/pulseboard/src/setup-surface.ts`
Setup wizard. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts`:

Imports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup`:
- `createAllowFromSection`, `createPromptParsedAllowFromForAccount`,
  `createStandardChannelSetupStatus`, `setSetupChannelEnabled`, `formatDocsLink`,
  `ChannelSetupWizard`, `ChannelSetupDmPolicy`

Key exports:
- `pulseboardSetupWizard: ChannelSetupWizard`:
  - `channel: "pulseboard"`
  - `status`: `createStandardChannelSetupStatus` — configured when `serverUrl && username`
  - `introNote`: explains server URL, API token, username, env vars (`PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL`,
    `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME`, `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN`), and boards config
  - `prepare`: checks env vars; offers to use them
  - `credentials: []` (no separate credential step; token via textInputs)
  - `textInputs`: prompts for server URL (`apiKey` key), API token (`token` key),
    username (`userId` key), default board list (`groupChannels` key, optional)
  - `allowFrom`: `createAllowFromSection` using `normalizePulseboardAllowEntry`
  - `dmPolicy`: `ChannelSetupDmPolicy` keying on `channels.pulseboard.dmPolicy`
  - `completionNote`: restart gateway, run `openclaw channels status --probe`
  - `disable`: `setSetupChannelEnabled(cfg, channel, false)`

---

### 16. `extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.ts`
Core plugin definition. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/channel.ts`.

Uses `createChatChannelPlugin` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`.

**Capabilities:**
```typescript
capabilities: {
  chatTypes: ["direct", "group"],
  media: true,
  blockStreaming: true,
}
```

**Config adapter** (`createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`):
- `sectionKey: "pulseboard"`, hooks up `listPulseboardAccountIds`,
  `resolvePulseboardAccount`, `resolveDefaultPulseboardAccountId`
- `clearBaseFields: ["name", "serverUrl", "apiToken", "username", "boards"]`
- `isConfigured: (account) => account.configured`
- `describeAccount`: via `describeAccountSnapshot` with `serverUrl`, `username`

**Messaging adapter:**
- `normalizeTarget: normalizePulseboardMessagingTarget`
- `targetResolver.looksLikeId: looksLikePulseboardTargetId`
- `targetResolver.hint: "<!board|username>"`

**Resolver** (`resolveTargets`):
- Groups: normalize to `!boardname` form; users: bare username
- Return resolved entries or errors for unresolvable inputs

**Directory** (`createChannelDirectoryAdapter`):
- `listPeers`: from `allowFrom` config entries, normalized as user IDs
- `listGroups`: from `boards` config list, normalized as board IDs

**Status** (`createComputedAccountStatusAdapter`):
- `buildChannelSummary`: spreads `buildBaseChannelStatusSummary`, adds `serverUrl`, `username`
- `probeAccount`: calls `probePulseboard`
- `resolveAccountSnapshot`: returns accountId, name, enabled, configured, serverUrl, username

**Gateway** (`startAccount`):
- Checks `account.configured`; logs start message
- Wraps `runStoppablePassiveMonitor` (stubbed monitor — PulseBoard is fictional, so the
  monitor function just waits for abort signal, representing an open WebSocket connection)

**Pairing** (text):
- `idLabel: "pulseboardUser"`, `message: PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE`
- `normalizeAllowEntry: normalizePulseboardAllowEntry`
- `notify`: sends a DM to the user via `sendMessagePulseboard`

**Security:**
- `resolveDmPolicy`: via `createScopedDmSecurityResolver`
- `collectWarnings`: via `composeAccountWarningCollectors` (no TLS warning needed; warns if
  `apiToken` is missing on a configured account)

**Outbound** (attached results):
```typescript
outbound: {
  base: {
    deliveryMode: "direct",
    chunker: chunkTextForOutbound,
    chunkerMode: "markdown",
    textChunkLimit: 4000,   // PulseBoard 4 000-char limit
  },
  attachedResults: {
    channel: "pulseboard",
    sendText: async ({ cfg, to, text, accountId, replyToId }) =>
      await sendMessagePulseboard(to, text, { cfg, accountId, replyTo: replyToId ?? undefined }),
    sendMedia: async ({ cfg, to, text, mediaUrl, accountId, replyToId }) =>
      await sendMessagePulseboard(to, mediaUrl ? `${text}\n\nAttachment: ${mediaUrl}` : text,
        { cfg, accountId, replyTo: replyToId ?? undefined }),
  },
}
```

tool_use_result.content:
# PulseBoard Channel Plugin — Implementation Plan

## Context

OpenClaw supports multiple messaging channels through a plugin/extension system. Each channel plugin lives under `extensions/<id>/` and implements the `ChannelPlugin` contract from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`. We need to add a new fictional "PulseBoard" channel — an imaginary real-time bulletin board platform where users post to named boards (group spaces, identified by `!boardname`) and send direct messages to each other. The plugin must support both `"direct"` and `"group"` chat types with a 4 000-character text chunk limit.

The implementation follows the same structure as the IRC plugin (the simplest complete channel plugin), with no modifications to any existing files. All 16 new files live exclusively under `extensions/pulseboard/`.

---

## Naming Invariants (enforced by `src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts`)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Directory | `extensions/pulseboard/` |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` → `id` | `"pulseboard"` |
| `package.json` → `name` | `"@openclaw/pulseboard"` |
| `package.json` → `openclaw.channel.id` | `"pulseboard"` |
| `npmSpec` | omit (bundled, not external) |

---

## File-by-File Plan

### 1. `extensions/pulseboard/package.json`
Mirror IRC's structure exactly. Key fields:
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/pulseboard",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw PulseBoard channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": ["./index.ts"],
    "install": { "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1" },
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "pulseboard",
      "label": "PulseBoard",
      "selectionLabel": "PulseBoard (Server + Token)",
      "detailLabel": "PulseBoard",
      "docsPath": "/channels/pulseboard",
      "docsLabel": "pulseboard",
      "blurb": "real-time bulletin board messaging with named boards and direct messages.",
      "aliases": ["pulse-board"],
      "systemImage": "rectangle.grid.2x2"
    }
  }
}
```
No extra `dependencies` block needed (no third-party runtime deps for a fictional protocol).

---

### 2. `extensions/pulseboard/openclaw.plugin.json`
```json
{
  "id": "pulseboard",
  "channels": ["pulseboard"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

---

### 3. `extensions/pulseboard/index.ts`
Main entry point — mirrors `extensions/irc/index.ts`.
```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseboardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setPulseboardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { pulseboardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setPulseboardRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "pulseboard",
  name: "PulseBoard",
  description: "PulseBoard channel plugin",
  plugin: pulseboardPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setPulseboardRuntime,
});
```

---

### 4. `extensions/pulseboard/setup-entry.ts`
Minimal setup-only entry — mirrors `extensions/irc/setup-entry.ts`.
```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { pulseboardPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseboardPlugin);
```

---

### 5. `extensions/pulseboard/api.ts`
Public API barrel (same two-line pattern as `extensions/irc/api.ts`):
```typescript
export * from "./src/accounts.js";
export * from "./src/setup-surface.js";
```

---

### 6. `extensions/pulseboard/src/types.ts`
Defines all PulseBoard-specific TypeScript types. Key types:

- **`PulseboardAccountConfig`** — per-account config stored in `channels.pulseboard.*`:
  - `name?`, `enabled?`, `serverUrl?`, `apiToken?`, `username?`, `boards?: string[]`,
    `dmPolicy?`, `allowFrom?`, `defaultTo?`, `textChunkLimit?`
- **`PulseboardConfig`** — top-level, extends account config with `accounts?` and `defaultAccount?`
- **`CoreConfig`** — `OpenClawConfig & { channels?: { pulseboard?: PulseboardConfig } }`
- **`PulseboardInboundMessage`** — `{ messageId, boardId: string|null, senderId, text, timestamp, isDirect }`
- **`PulseboardProbe`** — `BaseProbeResult<string> & { serverUrl, username, latencyMs? }`

Import `OpenClawConfig` and `BaseProbeResult` from `./runtime-api.js`; import `DmPolicy` etc. from `./runtime-api.js`.

---

### 7. `extensions/pulseboard/src/config-schema.ts`
Zod schema + `buildChannelConfigSchema`. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts`:

- Import `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape` from
  `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema`
- Import `z` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/zod`
- Define `PulseboardAccountSchemaBase` (strict) with all config fields
- `PulseboardConfigSchema` extends base with `accounts` and `defaultAccount`
- Export `PulseboardChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(PulseboardConfigSchema)`

---

### 8. `extensions/pulseboard/src/accounts.ts`
Account resolution. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts`:

- Import `createAccountListHelpers` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-helpers`
- Import `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID`, `normalizeAccountId` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-id`
- Import `resolveMergedAccountConfig` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/account-resolution`
- Import `normalizeResolvedSecretInputString` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/secret-input`

Key exports:
- `ResolvedPulseboardAccount` type — `{ accountId, enabled, name?, configured, serverUrl, username, apiToken, apiTokenSource: "env"|"config"|"none", config }`
- `listPulseboardAccountIds(cfg)` — via `createAccountListHelpers("pulseboard", ...)`
- `resolveDefaultPulseboardAccountId(cfg)` — via same helper
- `resolvePulseboardAccount({ cfg, accountId? })` — merges config, checks env vars
  (`PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME`, `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN`), returns resolved account.
  `configured = Boolean(serverUrl && username)`
- `listEnabledPulseboardAccounts(cfg)` — filters enabled accounts

---

### 9. `extensions/pulseboard/src/normalize.ts`
Target normalization for PulseBoard. Boards are prefixed with `!`, DMs use bare usernames.

Key exports:
- `isBoardTarget(target: string): boolean` — `target.startsWith("!")`
- `normalizePulseboardMessagingTarget(raw: string): string | undefined`
  - strips `"pulseboard:"` prefix
  - strips `"board:"` prefix, prepends `!` if missing
  - strips `"user:"` prefix
  - validates with `looksLikePulseboardTargetId`
- `looksLikePulseboardTargetId(raw: string): boolean`
  - boards: `!` + `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+`
  - users: `[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+`
- `normalizePulseboardAllowEntry(raw: string): string`
  - lowercases, strips `"pulseboard:"` and `"user:"` prefixes
- `normalizePulseboardAllowlist(entries?): string[]`

---

### 10. `extensions/pulseboard/src/runtime.ts`
Runtime store. Mirrors `extensions/irc/src/runtime.ts` exactly (3 lines of logic):
```typescript
import { createPluginRuntimeStore } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/runtime-store";
import type { PluginRuntime } from "./runtime-api.js";

const { setRuntime: setPulseboardRuntime, getRuntime: getPulseboardRuntime } =
  createPluginRuntimeStore<PluginRuntime>("PulseBoard runtime not initialized");
export { getPulseboardRuntime, setPulseboardRuntime };
export function clearPulseboardRuntime() {
  setPulseboardRuntime(undefined as unknown as PluginRuntime);
}
```

---

### 11. `extensions/pulseboard/src/runtime-api.ts`
Private runtime barrel. Since there is no dedicated `openclaw/plugin-sdk/pulseboard` facade,
re-export from the relevant general SDK subpaths directly. Needed by `channel.ts`, `runtime.ts`,
`send.ts`, `probe.ts`, and `types.ts`:

```typescript
// Private runtime barrel for the PulseBoard extension.
export {
  buildBaseChannelStatusSummary,
  createAccountStatusSink,
  createDefaultChannelRuntimeState,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/status-helpers";
export { DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/routing";
export {
  chunkTextForOutbound,
  getChatChannelMeta,
  PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
export type {
  BaseProbeResult,
  ChannelPlugin,
  OpenClawConfig,
  PluginRuntime,
  DmPolicy,
} from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
```

---

### 12. `extensions/pulseboard/src/send.ts`
Outbound send. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/send.ts`:

- `SendPulseboardResult = { messageId: string; target: string }`
- `sendMessagePulseboard(to, text, opts?)`:
  1. Load config from runtime or opts
  2. Call `resolvePulseboardAccount`
  3. Validate configured
  4. `normalizePulseboardMessagingTarget(to)` — throw if invalid
  5. Stub the HTTP call: in a real implementation this would `POST /api/messages` to
     `account.serverUrl` with `Authorization: Bearer <apiToken>` header
  6. Generate `messageId` with `makePulseboardMessageId()` — `pb-${Date.now()}-<random>`
  7. Call `runtime.channel.activity.record(...)` (wrapped in try/catch same as IRC)
  8. Return `{ messageId, target }`

---

### 13. `extensions/pulseboard/src/probe.ts`
Connectivity probe. Pattern from IRC's `probe.ts`:

- `probePulseboard(cfg, { accountId?, timeoutMs? }): Promise<PulseboardProbe>`
  - Resolve account; if not configured return `{ ok: false, error: "...", serverUrl, username }`
  - Stub: in a real implementation would `HEAD <serverUrl>/api/health` with a timeout
  - Return `{ ok: true, serverUrl, username, latencyMs: undefined }`

---

### 14. `extensions/pulseboard/src/setup-core.ts`
Setup adapter + config helpers. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/setup-core.ts`:

Imports from:
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-setup` — `ChannelSetupAdapter`, `ChannelSetupInput`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/config-runtime` — `DmPolicy`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/routing` — `normalizeAccountId`
- `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup` — `applyAccountNameToChannelSection`,
  `createSetupInputPresenceValidator`, `createTopLevelChannelAllowFromSetter`,
  `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicySetter`, `patchScopedAccountConfig`

Key exports:
- `updatePulseboardAccountConfig(cfg, accountId, patch)` — via `patchScopedAccountConfig`
- `setPulseboardDmPolicy(cfg, dmPolicy)` — via `createTopLevelChannelDmPolicySetter`
- `setPulseboardAllowFrom(cfg, allowFrom)` — via `createTopLevelChannelAllowFromSetter`
- `pulseboardSetupAdapter: ChannelSetupAdapter`:
  - `resolveAccountId`: `normalizeAccountId(accountId)`
  - `applyAccountName`: `applyAccountNameToChannelSection`
  - `validateInput`: `createSetupInputPresenceValidator` requiring `serverUrl`/`apiKey` and `token`/`userId`
  - `applyAccountConfig`: applies `serverUrl` (from `input.apiKey`), `apiToken` (from `input.token`), `username` (from `input.userId`) into config

---

### 15. `extensions/pulseboard/src/setup-surface.ts`
Setup wizard. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts`:

Imports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/setup`:
- `createAllowFromSection`, `createPromptParsedAllowFromForAccount`,
  `createStandardChannelSetupStatus`, `setSetupChannelEnabled`, `formatDocsLink`,
  `ChannelSetupWizard`, `ChannelSetupDmPolicy`

Key exports:
- `pulseboardSetupWizard: ChannelSetupWizard`:
  - `channel: "pulseboard"`
  - `status`: `createStandardChannelSetupStatus` — configured when `serverUrl && username`
  - `introNote`: explains server URL, API token, username, env vars (`PULSEBOARD_SERVER_URL`,
    `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME`, `PULSEBOARD_API_TOKEN`), and boards config
  - `prepare`: checks env vars; offers to use them
  - `credentials: []` (no separate credential step; token via textInputs)
  - `textInputs`: prompts for server URL (`apiKey` key), API token (`token` key),
    username (`userId` key), default board list (`groupChannels` key, optional)
  - `allowFrom`: `createAllowFromSection` using `normalizePulseboardAllowEntry`
  - `dmPolicy`: `ChannelSetupDmPolicy` keying on `channels.pulseboard.dmPolicy`
  - `completionNote`: restart gateway, run `openclaw channels status --probe`
  - `disable`: `setSetupChannelEnabled(cfg, channel, false)`

---

### 16. `extensions/pulseboard/src/channel.ts`
Core plugin definition. Pattern from `extensions/irc/src/channel.ts`.

Uses `createChatChannelPlugin` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`.

**Capabilities:**
```typescript
capabilities: {
  chatTypes: ["direct", "group"],
  media: true,
  blockStreaming: true,
}
```

**Config adapter** (`createScopedChannelConfigAdapter`):
- `sectionKey: "pulseboard"`, hooks up `listPulseboardAccountIds`,
  `resolvePulseboardAccount`, `resolveDefaultPulseboardAccountId`
- `clearBaseFields: ["name", "serverUrl", "apiToken", "username", "boards"]`
- `isConfigured: (account) => account.configured`
- `describeAccount`: via `describeAccountSnapshot` with `serverUrl`, `username`

**Messaging adapter:**
- `normalizeTarget: normalizePulseboardMessagingTarget`
- `targetResolver.looksLikeId: looksLikePulseboardTargetId`
- `targetResolver.hint: "<!board|username>"`

**Resolver** (`resolveTargets`):
- Groups: normalize to `!boardname` form; users: bare username
- Return resolved entries or errors for unresolvable inputs

**Directory** (`createChannelDirectoryAdapter`):
- `listPeers`: from `allowFrom` config entries, normalized as user IDs
- `listGroups`: from `boards` config list, normalized as board IDs

**Status** (`createComputedAccountStatusAdapter`):
- `buildChannelSummary`: spreads `buildBaseChannelStatusSummary`, adds `serverUrl`, `username`
- `probeAccount`: calls `probePulseboard`
- `resolveAccountSnapshot`: returns accountId, name, enabled, configured, serverUrl, username

**Gateway** (`startAccount`):
- Checks `account.configured`; logs start message
- Wraps `runStoppablePassiveMonitor` (stubbed monitor — PulseBoard is fictional, so the
  monitor function just waits for abort signal, representing an open WebSocket connection)

**Pairing** (text):
- `idLabel: "pulseboardUser"`, `message: PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE`
- `normalizeAllowEntry: normalizePulseboardAllowEntry`
- `notify`: sends a DM to the user via `sendMessagePulseboard`

**Security:**
- `resolveDmPolicy`: via `createScopedDmSecurityResolver`
- `collectWarnings`: via `composeAccountWarningCollectors` (no TLS warning needed; warns if
  `apiToken` is missing on a configured account)

**Outbound** (attached results):
```typescript
outbound: {
  base: {
    deliveryMode: "direct",
    chunker: chunkTextForOutbound,
    chunkerMode: "markdown",
    textChunkLimit: 4000,   // PulseBoard 4 000-char limit
  },
  attachedResults: {
    channel: "pulseboard",
    sendText: async ({ cfg, to, text, accountId, replyToId }) =>
      await sendMessagePulseboard(to, text, { cfg, accountId, replyTo: replyToId ?? undefined }),
    sendMedia: async ({ cfg, to, text, mediaUrl, accountId, replyToId }) =>
      await sendMessagePulseboard(to, mediaUrl ? `${text}\n\nAttachment: ${mediaUrl}` : text,
        { cfg, accountId, replyTo: replyToId ?? undefined }),
  },
}
```

---

## Critical Files Being Referenced (read-only)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `extensions/irc/package.json` | Package structure template |
| `extensions/irc/openclaw.plugin.json` | Manifest template |
| `extensions/irc/index.ts` | Entry point pattern |
| `extensions/irc/setup-entry.ts` | Setup entry pattern |
| `extensions/irc/api.ts` | Public barrel pattern |
| `extensions/irc/src/channel.ts` | `createChatChannelPlugin` usage |
| `extensions/irc/src/types.ts` | Type structure |
| `extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts` | Zod schema pattern |
| `extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts` | Account resolution pattern |
| `extensions/irc/src/runtime.ts` | Runtime store pattern |
| `extensions/irc/src/runtime-api.ts` | Runtime barrel pattern |
| `extensions/irc/src/normalize.ts` | Target normalization pattern |
| `extensions/irc/src/send.ts` | Outbound send pattern |
| `extensions/irc/src/setup-core.ts` | Setup adapter pattern |
| `extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts` | Setup wizard pattern |
| `src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts` | Naming invariant rules |
| `pnpm-workspace.yaml` | Workspace: `extensions/*` already included |

## SDK Subpaths Used

All imports in new files stay within `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`:

| Subpath | Used for |
|---|---|
| `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` | `createChatChannelPlugin`, `defineChannelPluginEntry`, `defineSetupPluginEntry`, `getChatChannelMeta`, `chunkTextForOutbound`, `PAIRING_APPROVED_MESSAGE`, `ChannelPlugin`, `OpenClawConfig` |
| `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-config-schema` | `buildChannelConfigSchema`, `DmPolicySchema`, `ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape` |

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
